reply:
[[BLOCK:slide35_shape1]]
AIC, AICc, and Bayesian Information Criterion (BIC) aim to produce a simplified model by penalizing complexity; however, simplicity is not the sole criterion. That being said, Elastic NET does not necessarily result in a model with the lowest AICc or BIC. Instead, it represents a balanced approach. LASSO and Ridge employ distinct penalization methods, while Elastic NET integrates both LASSO and Ridge penalties.  
The following brief article provides further clarification on the differences between AIC and BIC. Essentially, AIC is more suitable for cross-validation, whereas BIC excels in estimating the true model. In certain scenarios, AIC may be overly optimistic; thus, when conducting market segmentation in business contexts, BIC might be the preferred choice.  
We will discuss this further in the unit titled "Cluster Analysis."  
http://www.differencebetween.net/miscellaneous/difference-between-aic-and-bic/
